seo.title: AI Agent Observability in Google ADK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent observability in Google ADK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Google ADK, AI Agent Observability challenges arise from multi-agent delegation loops, Gemini model inconsistencies, session state drift in long conversations, and tool format mismatches between agents. These issues manifest as silent failures in agent workflows, making debugging complex due to ADK's asynchronous delegation patterns and shared session contexts. Vex integration via before_agent_callback provides real-time tracing to surface these hidden failures.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent A delegates to Agent B, which delegates back to Agent A indefinitely
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loops consume API quotas and timeout sessions without error

howItManifests.scenario: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
howItManifests.symptom: Different agents use gemini-1.5-pro vs gemini-1.5-flash inconsistently
howItManifests.impact: Format mismatches break tool parsing across agent handoffs

howItManifests.scenario: Session state corruption in long conversations
howItManifests.symptom: Shared session context gets overwritten by parallel agent executions
howItManifests.impact: Agents reference stale or missing conversation history

howItManifests.scenario: Tool response format mismatches across agents
howItManifests.symptom: One agent returns JSON, another returns plain text for same tool
howItManifests.impact: Downstream agents fail to parse tool responses correctly

detection.description: Validates all agents use consistent Gemini model versions

detection.description: Tracks session state mutations to detect corruption patterns

fixes.name: Delegation Circuit Breaker
fixes.description: Prevents circular delegation with max delegation depth

fixes.name: Unified Model Configuration
fixes.description: Enforces consistent model across all agents in workflow

fixes.name: Structured Tool Response Wrapper
fixes.description: Standardizes tool outputs across all agents

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Observability Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as a before_agent_callback in Google ADK, automatically tracing multi-agent interactions, delegation paths, model usage, and session state changes. It detects circular dependencies in real-time, flags model inconsistencies before they cascade, and monitors session drift across long conversations without manual instrumentation.

Vex's agent-specific detectors capture ADK's unique failure patterns like tool format mismatches during handoffs and delegation loops, providing structured alerts with remediation paths. The platform automatically suggests fixes like circuit breakers and model normalization, reducing MTTR from hours to seconds.

Installation is simple: pip install google-adk vex-sdk, then add Vex to your workflow callbacks for immediate observability across all agents.
vexAutomation.code:
from adk import Agent, Workflow
from vex_sdk import VexObserver

# Initialize Vex observer
vex = VexObserver(api_key='your-vex-api-key')

# Add Vex as before_agent callback
agent = Agent(
    name='researcher',
    model='gemini-1.5-pro',
    before_agent_callback=vex.trace_agent_call,
    after_agent_callback=vex.record_agent_output
)

workflow = Workflow(agents=[agent])

# Vex auto-detects all 4 failure modes
@vex.detector('circular_delegation')
def check_loops(trace):
    return len(trace.delegation_path) > 10

@vex.detector('model_consistency')
def check_models(trace):
    return len(set(a.model for a in trace.agents)) > 1

result = await workflow.run('Analyze market trends')

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK now to eliminate Google ADK observability blindspots in 5 minutes
